questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word: REBATE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is INCREASE. Rebate is a sum of money that is given back to you because you have paid too much. Increase is the correct antonym of the given word Rebate. Deduction: An amount or part taken away from a total. Bonus: A payment that is added to what is usual.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. Freedom fighters laboured / to the good of / the poor enslaved masses
questions.explanation: Second segment contains error. Laboured for means to make efforts for something. Hence the correct statement is- Freedom fighters laboured for the good of the poor enslaved masses.

questions.question: Arrange the statements in the correct order to make a meaningful paragraph. A. Impressed with Jane’s patience with animals, he chose her for the mission to understand chimpanzees. B. In 1957, she went to Kenya where she met the British paleoanthropologist, Louis Leakey. C. As a child she would wait for hours to observe a chicken laying an egg. D. Jane Goodall grew up in the English town of Bournemouth.
questions.explanation: The correct arrangement is DCBA. The paragraph will start from the proper noun Which means Jane Goodall. Then the paragraph talks about his childhood and arranged in chronological order. Hence the correct arrangement of the given statement are- Jane Goodall grew up in the English town of Bournemouth. As a child she would wait for hours to observe a chicken laying an egg. In 1957, she went to Kenya where she met the British paleoanthropologist, Louis Leakey. Impressed with Jane’s patience with animals, he chose her for the mission to understand chimpanzees.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Achille’s heel
options: A fatal weakness in spite of overall strength
A secret weapon to be used as a last resort
A past deed which one is trying to hide
The person who is blamed if anything goes wrong
questions.explanation: Option a is the correct answer. The term Achilles heel references a vulnerability or weakness. It means a small problem or weakness in a person or system that can result in failure.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. So dangerous did the weather conditions ______ that all mountain roads were closed.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is become. Have become, became and becomes are irrelevant because in sentence did is already used hence the sentence will follow the rule of Simple Past tense.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The coloured clothes should not be washed with the whites.
options: You should not have washed the coloured clothes with the whites.
Do not washed the coloured clothes with the whites.
Do not be washing the coloured clothes with the whites.
You should not wash the coloured clothes with the whites.
questions.explanation: Option d is the correct active form of the given sentence. Rule: Active – Passive: Sub + Should + v1 + Object ---- Object + should be + v3 + obj.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option c is the correct answer. Pathetic is the correct spelling which means causing you to feel pity or sadness. Adventure, Acquire, Benefit are correctly spelt.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Triumphant
questions.explanation: The appropriate synonym of TRIUMPHANT is Victorious. Triumphant: feeling or showing great happiness because you have won or succeeded at something. Fortunate: Lucky. Affluent: Having a lot of money or possession; rich. Pompous: showing that you think you are more important than other people, for example by using long words that sound impressive.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Holiday homework / are essential to keeping / students busy / during the vacation.
questions.explanation: Option a is the correct answer. The given sentence is grammatically incorrect. The correct sentence is: Holiday homework is essential to keep students busy during the vacation.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. RAVAGE
questions.explanation: Option c is the correct answer. Ravage means to damage something very badly; to destroy something. Surrender: to stop fighting and admit that you have lost Construct: to build or make something.Damage: harm or injury caused when something is broken or spoiled. Liberate: to allow somebody/something to be free

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Handicrafts are / export from India / to several / European countries.
questions.explanation: The given sentence is grammatically incorrect. It is in Passive form and in Passive form—is/am/are + v3 is used. Hence the correct sentence is: Handicrafts are exported from India to several European countries.

questions.question: In the following question, the sentence given with a blank to be filled in with an appropriate word. Select the correct alternative out of the four and indicate it by selecting the appropriate option. The survey provided the company with a wide ______ of feedback on its product.
questions.explanation: Spectrum suits the best. Spectrum means a range of objects, ideas, or opinions. Option, choices and colours are not suitable.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A spear used for hunting large fish
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Harpoon. It is a long-pointed weapon with a long rope attached to it, which is fired or thrown by people hunting whales or large sea fish.Lance: a long weapon with a wooden shaft and a pointed steel head, formerly used by a horseman in charging. Dagger: a type of knife used as a weapon, especially in past times.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. REBUKE
questions.explanation: Option d is the correct answer. Rebuke: an act or expression of criticism and censure. Flattery: excessive or insincere praise. Admonition, Censure, Scold are antonym of the given word Rebuke.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A room or large cupboard for storing food
questions.explanation: Larder is the correct answer. Larder: a large cupboard or small room that is used for storing food Bakery: a place where bread, cakes, etc. are baked to be sold Wardrobe: a large cupboard in which you can hang yours clotheAlmirah: A cupboard or wardrobe that is not fixed to a wall.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the highlighted idiom in the given sentence. It was a lavish birthday party and I could see my friend e̲a̲t̲ ̲l̲i̲k̲e̲ ̲a̲ ̲h̲o̲r̲s̲e̲.̲
questions.explanation: Option c is the correct answer. Eat like a horse: to eat a lot because you have a large appetite; eating excessively.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. She said to me, “I saw this old man long ago.”
options: She told me that she had seen that old man long before.
She told to me that she had seen that old man long before.
She said me that she had seen this old man long ago.
She said to me that she had seen that old man long ago.
questions.explanation: Option a is the correct answer. Rule to change direct form to indirect form: If reporting verb is in past tense and reported speech is also in past tense then-------- Simple past tense changes into Past perfect tense. Past continuous tense into Past Perfect Continuous. Also this is changed into that. Hence the correct indirect form of the given sentence is- She told me that she had seen that old man long before.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Varun ate six rotis at dinner.
options: At dinner, six rotis was ate by Varun.
At dinner, six rotis were eaten by Varun.
At dinner, Varun had eaten six rotis.
At dinner, had Varun eaten six rotis.
questions.explanation: Option b is the correct answer. Rule: Sub + v2 + obj ---- Obj + was/were + v3 + by + obj. Hence the correct passive voice is- At dinner, six rotis were eaten by Varun.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain a grammatical error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. Father was upset / when he found that / you are not there.
questions.explanation: Option d is the correct answer. The sentence is in past tense. Hence are is incorrect. The correct sentence is: Father was upset when he found that you were not there.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the bold segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. W̲o̲u̲l̲d̲ ̲y̲o̲u̲ ̲h̲a̲v̲e̲ ̲b̲e̲e̲n̲ so kind as to close the window?
questions.explanation: Option b is the correct answer. Would you be will replace Would you have been.Would is used when we want to politely request someone for something. And model Would is always followed by v1.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The promise of nuclear power has so 1. ---------- outweighed all of these concerns, and India has reason to be proud of its technology and determination to look for non-fossil 2. -------------- in its energy planning. However, 3. ------------------ rapid progress in technology in other 4. -------------- energy sources such as wind and solar power, the collapse of oil prices and the expansion in gas projects as a viable and clean alternative, that promise 5. --------------------- Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: Option a is the correct answer. So far is the appropriate option to fill in the blank which means until now.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The promise of nuclear power has so 1. ---------- outweighed all of these concerns, and India has reason to be proud of its technology and determination to look for non-fossil 2. -------------- in its energy planning. However, 3. ------------------ rapid progress in technology in other 4. -------------- energy sources such as wind and solar power, the collapse of oil prices and the expansion in gas projects as a viable and clean alternative, that promise 5. --------------------- Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Alternative which means that you can use, do, etc. instead of something else. Compulsion: the act of forcing somebody to do something or being forced to do something. Obligation: the state of having to do something because it is a law or duty, or because you have promised Constraints: something that limits you.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The promise of nuclear power has so 1. ---------- outweighed all of these concerns, and India has reason to be proud of its technology and determination to look for non-fossil 2. -------------- in its energy planning. However, 3. ------------------ rapid progress in technology in other 4. -------------- energy sources such as wind and solar power, the collapse of oil prices and the expansion in gas projects as a viable and clean alternative, that promise 5. --------------------- Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: with is the appropriate word to fill in the blank.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The promise of nuclear power has so 1. ---------- outweighed all of these concerns, and India has reason to be proud of its technology and determination to look for non-fossil 2. -------------- in its energy planning. However, 3. ------------------ rapid progress in technology in other 4. -------------- energy sources such as wind and solar power, the collapse of oil prices and the expansion in gas projects as a viable and clean alternative, that promise 5. --------------------- Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: renewable is the appropriate word to fill in the blank. In the sentence wind and solar power word is used and they are renewable energy. Renewable energy: that will always exist, that can be continued or replaced with a new one for another period of time.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The promise of nuclear power has so 1. ---------- outweighed all of these concerns, and India has reason to be proud of its technology and determination to look for non-fossil 2. -------------- in its energy planning. However, 3. ------------------ rapid progress in technology in other 4. -------------- energy sources such as wind and solar power, the collapse of oil prices and the expansion in gas projects as a viable and clean alternative, that promise 5. ---------------------. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: Option d is the correct answer. Dim means not bright or easy to see; not clear.And in the sentence dim is used as a verb so the correct sentence is : Renewable energy sources such as wind and solar power, the collapse of oil prices and the expansion in gas projects as a viable and clean alternative, that promise has dimmed.
